description: Establish end-to-end observability across all backend microservices and the Next.js frontend. Covers distributed tracing via Micrometer Tracing and Zipkin, Prometheus metrics scraping with custom business counters, structured JSON logging enriched with MDC fields (traceId, spanId, correlationId, userId), Kafka trace context propagation via B3 headers, and structured client/server logging in Next.js via Pino. Local infrastructure (Zipkin, Prometheus, Grafana) is added to the docker-compose stack.
functionality: inspect distributed traces across all services, query Prometheus metrics, and correlate structured log lines end-to-end using traceId and correlationId
benefit: detect failures, diagnose latency in the onboarding pipeline, and support production incident investigation without ad-hoc debugging

stories.summary: Add Micrometer Tracing and Zipkin reporter to all backend services
stories.description:
Add distributed tracing to all four Spring Boot services using Micrometer Tracing with the Brave/Zipkin bridge. Each service exports spans to Zipkin. The existing X-Correlation-ID is bridged into Brave baggage so correlation IDs appear on every span. The api-gateway uses the WebFlux/reactive variant with reactor context propagation.

Affected services: <backend/user-service>, <backend/onboarding-service>, <backend/provisioning-service>, <backend/api-gateway>

Dependencies to add to each pom.xml:
```
<dependency>
  <groupId>io.micrometer</groupId>
  <artifactId>micrometer-tracing-bridge-brave</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
  <groupId>io.zipkin.reporter2</groupId>
  <artifactId>zipkin-reporter-brave</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

Application config to add:
```
management.zipkin.tracing.endpoint=http://localhost:9411/api/v2/spans
management.tracing.sampling.probability=1.0
```

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Spans appear in Zipkin for a registration request
acceptanceCriteria.given: a running Zipkin instance and all four services started with tracing configured
acceptanceCriteria.when: a POST /api/v1/registrations request flows through the api-gateway to user-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: a trace with child spans for api-gateway and user-service is visible in the Zipkin UI with the correct correlationId as a baggage field

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Existing X-Correlation-ID is preserved in trace baggage
acceptanceCriteria.given: a client request carrying an X-Correlation-ID header
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request is processed by any service
acceptanceCriteria.then: the correlationId baggage value on the resulting span matches the inbound X-Correlation-ID header

acceptanceCriteria.given: the api-gateway processes a proxied request reactively
acceptanceCriteria.when: the downstream service receives the forwarded request
acceptanceCriteria.then: the B3 trace headers are present and the downstream span is a child of the gateway span

tasks.summary: Add micrometer-tracing-bridge-brave and zipkin-reporter-brave to all four pom.xml files
tasks.description: Add the Brave bridge and Zipkin reporter dependencies to <backend/user-service/pom.xml>, <backend/onboarding-service/pom.xml>, <backend/provisioning-service/pom.xml>, and <backend/api-gateway/pom.xml>.

tasks.summary: Configure Zipkin exporter endpoint in each application.yml
tasks.description: Add management.zipkin.tracing.endpoint and management.tracing.sampling.probability=1.0 to each service's <application.yml>. Use an environment variable override for non-local environments.

tasks.summary: Bridge X-Correlation-ID into Brave baggage
tasks.description: In each service's correlation ID servlet filter (or gateway filter for api-gateway), write the correlationId into a Brave baggage field using BaggageField so it propagates with every outbound span.

tasks.summary: Verify reactor context propagation in api-gateway
tasks.description: Confirm that micrometer-tracing reactor context hooks are active in the api-gateway WebFlux application and that child spans are created correctly for proxied routes.

tasks.summary: Add unit and integration tests for trace propagation
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that B3 headers are forwarded by Feign interceptors and that the correlationId baggage field is populated on outbound calls.

stories.summary: Expose Prometheus metrics endpoint on all backend services
stories.description:
Add the Micrometer Prometheus registry to all four services so that Prometheus can scrape the /actuator/prometheus endpoint. Expose standard Spring Boot metrics (JVM, HTTP, Kafka, datasource) and add custom business counters for registration requests, onboarding step failures, and provisioning outcomes.

Dependency to add to each pom.xml:
```
<dependency>
  <groupId>io.micrometer</groupId>
  <artifactId>micrometer-registry-prometheus</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

Actuator config:
```
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,prometheus,metrics
management.endpoint.prometheus.enabled=true
```

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Prometheus endpoint is reachable and returns metrics
acceptanceCriteria.given: a running service with micrometer-registry-prometheus configured
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 200 with content-type text/plain and contains standard JVM and HTTP server metrics

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Custom registration counter increments on each registration
acceptanceCriteria.given: the user-service is running with a custom counter named onboarding_registrations_total
acceptanceCriteria.then: the counter value increments by 1 and is visible in the /actuator/prometheus scrape output

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Onboarding step failure counter increments on failed steps
acceptanceCriteria.given: the onboarding-service has a custom counter named onboarding_step_failures_total tagged with step_type
acceptanceCriteria.when: an onboarding step transitions to FAILED state
acceptanceCriteria.then: the counter increments and the step_type tag reflects the failed step

tasks.summary: Add micrometer-registry-prometheus to all four pom.xml files
tasks.description: Add the Prometheus registry dependency to <backend/user-service/pom.xml>, <backend/onboarding-service/pom.xml>, <backend/provisioning-service/pom.xml>, and <backend/api-gateway/pom.xml>.

tasks.summary: Configure actuator to expose the prometheus endpoint in each application.yml
tasks.description: Set management.endpoints.web.exposure.include to include prometheus and configure management.endpoint.prometheus.enabled=true in each service's <application.yml>.

tasks.summary: Add custom business counters in user-service and onboarding-service
tasks.description: Register a MeterRegistry counter onboarding_registrations_total in user-service and onboarding_step_failures_total (tagged by step_type) and provisioning_outcomes_total (tagged by provider and result) in onboarding-service and provisioning-service.

tasks.summary: Add tests verifying counter increments
tasks.description: Use SimpleMeterRegistry in unit tests to assert that registration and step-failure counters increment correctly on the relevant service method calls.

stories.summary: Structured JSON logging with MDC enrichment across all backend services
stories.description:
Replace the default Logback pattern layout with a JSON encoder using logstash-logback-encoder. Every log line includes standard fields plus MDC context: traceId, spanId, correlationId, userId, and service name. The existing correlation ID servlet/gateway filter already writes correlationId to MDC; this story adds the remaining fields and the JSON encoder.

Dependency to add to each pom.xml:
```
<dependency>
  <groupId>net.logstash.logback</groupId>
  <artifactId>logstash-logback-encoder</artifactId>
  <version>8.0</version>
</dependency>
```

Affected config files: <backend/user-service/src/main/resources/logback-spring.xml>, and equivalents in each other service.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Log lines are emitted as JSON
acceptanceCriteria.then: the output is a single-line JSON object containing level, message, logger, timestamp, and thread fields

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Trace context fields appear on every log line during a request
acceptanceCriteria.given: Micrometer Tracing is active and a request is being processed
acceptanceCriteria.when: any logger within the request thread emits a log statement
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON log line includes non-empty traceId, spanId, and correlationId fields matching the active trace

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: userId is present on authenticated request log lines
acceptanceCriteria.given: a JWT-authenticated request is processed by user-service or onboarding-service
acceptanceCriteria.when: a service method logs during that request
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON log line includes a userId field populated from the JWT subject claim

tasks.summary: Add logstash-logback-encoder dependency to all four pom.xml files
tasks.description: Add net.logstash.logback:logstash-logback-encoder version 8.0 to each service's pom.xml.

tasks.summary: Create logback-spring.xml with LogstashEncoder in each service
tasks.description: Add or replace <backend/{service}/src/main/resources/logback-spring.xml> with a configuration using LogstashEncoder. Include a dev profile that uses pretty-print and a prod profile that emits raw JSON.

tasks.summary: Enrich correlation ID servlet filter to write traceId, spanId, and userId to MDC
tasks.description: Update each service's correlation ID filter to also set traceId and spanId MDC keys from the active Brave span (via Tracer.currentSpan()) and userId from the Spring Security context.

tasks.summary: Add service name as a static MDC field
tasks.description: Configure each service's logback-spring.xml to include a static service field using the spring.application.name property so log lines are identifiable across aggregated log streams.

tasks.summary: Add tests verifying MDC fields are present in log output
tasks.description: Write integration tests using a captured log appender to assert that traceId, correlationId, and service fields are non-empty on log lines emitted during a request.

stories.summary: Kafka trace context propagation and consumer metrics
stories.description:
Instrument Kafka producers and consumers with Micrometer Tracing so that trace context (B3 headers) is injected into Kafka record headers by producers and extracted by consumers, creating parent-child span relationships across async boundaries. Also enable Kafka consumer lag metrics via KafkaConsumerMetrics so Prometheus can alert on processing backlogs.

This builds on the Micrometer Brave bridge added in the tracing story. Spring Kafka 3.x + Micrometer auto-configures the ObservationRegistry on KafkaTemplate and listener containers when micrometer-tracing is on the classpath.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Kafka consumer span is a child of the producer span
acceptanceCriteria.given: Micrometer Tracing is active on both user-service (producer) and onboarding-service (consumer)
acceptanceCriteria.when: user-service publishes a UserRegisteredV1 event and onboarding-service processes it
acceptanceCriteria.then: the consumer span in Zipkin has the producer span as its parent, forming a single end-to-end trace

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Consumer lag metric is available in Prometheus
acceptanceCriteria.given: KafkaConsumerMetrics is registered on the MeterRegistry for onboarding-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: kafka_consumer_fetch_manager_records_lag metrics are present for each topic partition

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Correlation ID is preserved across the Kafka boundary
acceptanceCriteria.given: a request with a known X-Correlation-ID triggers an outbox event publication
acceptanceCriteria.when: the consumer processes the Kafka message
acceptanceCriteria.then: the consumer's MDC correlationId matches the value set by the original request

tasks.summary: Enable KafkaTemplate observation in user-service, onboarding-service, and provisioning-service
tasks.description: Set spring.kafka.template.observation-enabled=true and spring.kafka.listener.observation-enabled=true in each service's <application.yml> to activate Micrometer tracing on Kafka producers and consumers.

tasks.summary: Register KafkaConsumerMetrics bean in each Kafka consumer service
tasks.description: Add a KafkaConsumerMetrics bean wired to the MeterRegistry in onboarding-service and provisioning-service so consumer lag and offset metrics are exported to Prometheus.

tasks.summary: Propagate correlationId into Kafka record headers via outbox publisher
tasks.description: Update the outbox event publisher in each service to read the correlationId from MDC and write it as a Kafka record header, so consumers can restore it when processing the message.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for cross-service trace propagation via Kafka
tasks.description: Add a TestContainers-based integration test that publishes an event on one service and asserts that the consumer span shares the same traceId as the producer span.

stories.summary: Add Zipkin, Prometheus, and Grafana to local docker-compose stack
stories.description:
Extend <infra/docker/docker-compose.yml> with three new services: Zipkin (trace collector and UI), Prometheus (metrics scraper), and Grafana (dashboards). Add a <infra/docker/prometheus.yml> scrape config targeting the /actuator/prometheus endpoint of all four backend services. Include a starter Grafana dashboard provisioning config for JVM and HTTP metrics.

Port assignments (avoiding conflicts with Schema Registry on 9090):
- Zipkin: 9411
- Prometheus: 9093
- Grafana: 3001

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Zipkin UI is accessible after docker-compose up
acceptanceCriteria.given: the full docker-compose stack is started
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Zipkin search UI loads and accepts trace queries

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Prometheus scrapes all four backend services
acceptanceCriteria.given: all backend services and Prometheus are running
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Prometheus targets page at http://localhost:9093/targets is opened
acceptanceCriteria.then: all four service scrape targets show state UP

acceptanceCriteria.given: Grafana starts with the provisioning config
acceptanceCriteria.then: a Prometheus datasource pointing to http://prometheus:9093 is available without manual configuration

tasks.summary: Add Zipkin service to docker-compose.yml
tasks.description: Add openzipkin/zipkin:3 container with port 9411:9411 to <infra/docker/docker-compose.yml>.

tasks.summary: Add Prometheus service and scrape config to docker-compose.yml
tasks.description: Add prom/prometheus container on port 9093:9090 and mount a <infra/docker/prometheus.yml> scrape config that targets all four backend services at their actuator prometheus endpoints.

tasks.summary: Add Grafana service with Prometheus datasource provisioning
tasks.description: Add grafana/grafana container on port 3001:3000 with volume-mounted datasource and dashboard provisioning configs under <infra/docker/grafana/>.

tasks.summary: Update infra/.env.example with new service URLs
tasks.description: Add ZIPKIN_ENDPOINT, PROMETHEUS_URL, and GRAFANA_URL entries to <infra/docker/.env.example> so developers can override them for non-local environments.

tasks.summary: Update README or CLAUDE.md local infrastructure table
tasks.description: Add Zipkin (9411), Prometheus (9093), and Grafana (3001) rows to the local infrastructure reference table in the project docs.

stories.summary: Structured logging in Next.js frontend with Pino
stories.description:
Add structured logging to the Next.js frontend using Pino. Server-side request/response logging uses pino-http as middleware in the Next.js server instrumentation file. Client-side uncaught errors and key navigation events use the pino/browser build. All log lines include the X-Correlation-ID forwarded from the backend response or generated client-side. In development, pino-pretty formats output for readability.

Affected area: <frontend/apps/web>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Server-side requests are logged as structured JSON
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Next.js app is running in production mode
acceptanceCriteria.when: an HTTP request is handled by the Next.js server
acceptanceCriteria.then: a single-line JSON log line is emitted containing method, url, statusCode, responseTime, and correlationId

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Client-side unhandled errors are captured and logged
acceptanceCriteria.given: the pino browser logger is initialized with an error boundary or window.onerror hook
acceptanceCriteria.when: an unhandled JavaScript error occurs in the browser
acceptanceCriteria.then: a structured log event with level error, message, stack, and correlationId is captured and forwarded to the server log endpoint or console

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Correlation ID flows from backend response into client log lines
acceptanceCriteria.given: the backend returns an X-Correlation-ID response header on API calls
acceptanceCriteria.when: subsequent client-side log events are emitted for that user session
acceptanceCriteria.then: the correlationId field in those log lines matches the value from the backend response header

acceptanceCriteria.when: the dev server handles a request
acceptanceCriteria.then: server log output is human-readable and colour-coded rather than raw JSON

tasks.summary: Add pino, pino-http, and pino-pretty to frontend/apps/web
tasks.description: Run pnpm add pino pino-http and pnpm add -D pino-pretty inside <frontend/apps/web>.

tasks.summary: Create a server logger module using pino with environment-aware transport
tasks.description: Add <frontend/apps/web/lib/logger/server.ts> that exports a pino instance configured with pino-pretty in development and raw JSON in production. Include a default service field set to web.

tasks.summary: Wire pino-http middleware in Next.js instrumentation
tasks.description: Create or update <frontend/apps/web/instrumentation.ts> to register pino-http as a request logger for the Node.js server runtime, capturing method, url, statusCode, responseTime, and correlationId.

tasks.summary: Create a browser logger module using pino/browser
tasks.description: Add <frontend/apps/web/lib/logger/client.ts> using the pino/browser export with a transmit handler that POSTs log events to a /api/log endpoint for server-side aggregation.

tasks.summary: Capture client-side unhandled errors and route them through the browser logger
tasks.description: Add a global error boundary component and a window.onerror/unhandledrejection hook in the root layout that feeds errors into the browser logger with correlationId context.

tasks.summary: Extract and store correlationId from backend API responses into logger context
tasks.description: Update the frontend API client in <frontend/apps/web/lib> to read the X-Correlation-ID response header and store it in a request-scoped context that the browser logger reads for each log event.
